title: ANATOMY OF A BITCOIN WALLET
subtitle: A wallet is not one thing. It's a small system that does five jobs at once.
lead: A Bitcoin wallet is software (or hardware) that holds the parts you need to prove ownership and move bitcoin. Open one up and you'll find five components — each doing distinct work.

components.name: Random number generator
components.does: Produces the entropy that becomes the seed.
components.if_fails: A weak RNG means the seed is guessable from day one. Everything built on top is compromised.

components.name: Seed phrase storage
components.does: Turns the entropy + checksum into 12 or 24 readable words. The human backup.
components.if_fails: Lose the seed phrase and you lose every key the wallet ever derives.

components.name: Key derivation engine
components.does: Turns one seed into a tree of private keys. One seed → millions of keys.
components.if_fails: Same 12 words, different derivation paths = different wallets. Not a contradiction, a configuration.

components.name: Signing engine
components.does: Uses a private key to sign a transaction. Proof you have the right to spend.
components.if_fails: The wallet can read balances but cannot move funds.

components.name: Network interface
components.does: Broadcasts signed transactions, queries balances, watches addresses for incoming payments.
components.if_fails: The wallet can sign but cannot transmit. Intentional on air-gapped signing devices.

arc.caption: Five jobs in one chain. Strong randomness becomes a backup, becomes a key tree, becomes a signature, becomes a transaction. Every step depends on the one before.

misconceptions.better: Bitcoin lives on the network. Your wallet stores the keys that let you move it.

misconceptions.wrong: Hot vs cold is the only safety distinction that matters.
misconceptions.better: The components matter more. A cold wallet with a weak RNG isn't safe; a hot wallet with strong key isolation can be safer than you'd guess.

misconceptions.wrong: All wallets that take 12 words are equivalent.
misconceptions.better: Different wallets implement different derivation paths, address types, and passphrase models. Same words ≠ same wallet.

misconceptions.wrong: My wallet's password protects my bitcoin.
misconceptions.better: The password protects access to the wallet software on one device. The seed phrase is what protects the bitcoin across all devices, all time.

key_lesson: Five parts. One job: prove you can move bitcoin without giving away how.
